mission.title: Install and Configure Envoy on Kubernetes
mission.description: Envoy is a cloud-native high-performance edge/middle/service proxy that helps manage microservices traffic efficiently. Installing Envoy allows you to leverage its advanced features for load balancing, service discovery, and observability in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Install Envoy via official Docker image
steps.description:
Run Envoy as a standalone proxy:
```bash
docker pull envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3
docker run -d --name envoy -p 10000:10000 -p 9901:9901 envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3
```
Or install the binary:
```bash
# macOS
brew install envoy

# Linux (from GetEnvoy)
curl -L https://func-e.io/install.sh | bash -s -- -b /usr/local/bin
func-e run --version
```

steps.title: Deploy Envoy on Kubernetes
steps.description:
Deploy Envoy as a sidecar or standalone proxy:
```bash
kubectl create namespace envoy
kubectl apply -n envoy -f - << 'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: envoy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: envoy
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: envoy
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: envoy
        image: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3
        ports:
        - containerPort: 10000
        - containerPort: 9901
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: envoy
spec:
  selector:
    app: envoy
  ports:
  - name: proxy
    port: 10000
  - name: admin
    port: 9901
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check Envoy is running and access the admin interface:
```bash
# Docker
curl http://localhost:9901/server_info

# Kubernetes
kubectl get pods -n envoy
kubectl port-forward svc/envoy -n envoy 9901:9901
curl http://localhost:9901/server_info
```

uninstall.title: Remove Envoy
uninstall.description:
```bash
# Docker
docker stop envoy && docker rm envoy

# Kubernetes
kubectl delete namespace envoy

# Binary
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/envoy
```

upgrade.title: Update to latest version
upgrade.description:
```bash
# Docker
docker pull envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3
docker stop envoy && docker rm envoy
docker run -d --name envoy -p 10000:10000 -p 9901:9901 envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3

# Kubernetes
kubectl set image deployment/envoy envoy=envoyproxy/envoy:v1.32.3 -n envoy
```

troubleshooting.title: Admin interface not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
The admin interface runs on port 9901 by default. Ensure it's exposed:
```bash
curl http://localhost:9901/help
```

troubleshooting.title: For production use, consider Istio or Contour
troubleshooting.description: Envoy is the data plane proxy used by service meshes like Istio and ingress controllers like Contour. For production Kubernetes use, installing Istio or Contour is recommended over standalone Envoy.

resolution.summary: Envoy proxy is installed and running as a standalone proxy with the admin interface accessible.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
